deadline: February 26, 2026 by no later than 3:00 p.m. (Large Project Reservation Applications); Small Project Rebate Applications: Accepted on a rolling basis, subject to funding availability (as of 03/18/2026, the FY26 Small Project set-aside rebate program is fully subscribed)
geographic_restrictions: Designated Downtown Development Districts in Delaware (Dover, Seaford, Wilmington, Georgetown, Harrington, Laurel, Milford, Smyrna, City of New Castle, Clayton, Delaware City, Middletown)
project_area: Commercial, industrial, residential, and mixed-use buildings or facilities within designated Downtown Development Districts
demographic_requirements: None specified; open to all qualified investors and property owners meeting site control and investment requirements
eligibility_summary: Investors must make Qualified Real Property Investments (QRPI) within the boundary of a designated DDD, in conformance with the approved District plan, and in excess of the Minimum Qualified Investment Threshold (MQIT). Investments must be made after the property location is officially incorporated into a designated District. Investors must meet site control requirements (e.g., legal title, sales agreement, or owner’s consent).
